title: Tools & Software
text:
All tools are freely available under permissive licences. Source code is on GitHub.





Prediction


  
    ⏱️
    
      DeepLC
      Retention time
    
  
  
    Predicts when peptides will elute from the LC column during a mass spectrometry run. Handles modified peptides accurately — even modifications never seen during training. Adapts to new instruments and setups through transfer learning, requiring only a small number of example peptides.
    
      ⬡ GitHub
      Docs
      PyPI
      Web app
    
    
      Bouwmeester et al. (2021). DeepLC can predict retention times for peptides that carry as-yet unseen modifications. Nature Methods. 10.1038/s41592-021-01301-5
      Bouwmeester, Nameni et al. (2026). Transfer learning in DeepLC improves LC retention time prediction. Nature Communications. 10.1038/s41467-026-68981-5
    
  
  
    Developers
    
      
        RB
        Robbin BouwmeesterLead developer
      
      
        RG
        Ralf GabrielsContributor
      
      
        SD
        Sven DegroeveContributor
      
    
  



  
    📊
    
      MS²PIP
      Fragment intensity
    
  
  
    Predicts fragment ion peak intensities from peptide sequence alone. Used to build in silico spectral libraries and to power the MS²Rescore rescoring pipeline. Fine-tuning for user-defined modifications is available through MS²DIP.
    
      ⬡ GitHub
      Web app
    
    
      Degroeve & Martens (2013). MS²PIP: a tool for MS/MS peak intensity prediction. Bioinformatics. 10.1093/bioinformatics/btt355
    
  
  
    Developers
    
      
        SD
        Sven DegroeveOriginal author
      
      
        AD
        Arthur DeclercqLead developer
      
    
  



  
    💨
    
      IM2Deep
      Ion mobility
    
  
  
    Predicts collisional cross-section (CCS) values for peptides, including multiply modified ones. Enables confident ion mobility filtering in DDA and DIA workflows.
    
      ⬡ GitHub
    
    
      Declercq et al. (2024). IM2Deep: predicting collisional cross sections for multi-modified peptides. Journal of Proteome Research. 10.1021/acs.jproteome.4c00098
    
  
  
    Developers
    
      
        AD
        Arthur DeclercqLead developer
      
      
        RB
        Robbin BouwmeesterContributor
      
    
  



Rescoring


  
    🏆
    
      MS²Rescore
      PSM rescoring
    
  
  
    Rescores peptide-spectrum matches by combining predicted LC-MS/MS features — retention time, fragment intensities, and ion mobility — with observed features. Substantially increases the number of confident identifications compared to search engine scores alone. Validated for immunopeptidomics and metaproteomics.
    
      ⬡ GitHub
      Docs
    
    
      Declercq et al. (2022). MS²Rescore: data-driven rescoring dramatically boosts immunopeptide identification rates. Molecular & Cellular Proteomics. 10.1016/j.mcpro.2022.100266
    
  
  
    Developers
    
      
        RG
        Ralf GabrielsLead developer
      
      
        AD
        Arthur DeclercqContributor
      
    
  



Data Standards & Utilities


  
    🔧
    
      psm_utils
      PSM handling
    
  
  
    A Python library for parsing, converting, and handling peptide-spectrum matches from any major search engine. Provides a unified PSM object model and format-agnostic I/O for building analysis pipelines.
    
      ⬡ GitHub
      Docs
      PyPI
    
  
  
    Developers
    
      
        RG
        Ralf GabrielsLead developer
      
    
  



  
    🗂️
    
      lesSDRF
      Metadata
    
  
  
    An interactive desktop app for annotating proteomics experiments in the SDRF format. Guides users through ontology-compliant metadata entry. HAMLET extends this with LLM-based pre-filling from manuscript text.
    
      ⬡ GitHub
      Web app
    
    
      Van Puyvelde et al. (2022). lesSDRF: a desktop app for SDRF proteomics metadata annotation. Journal of Proteome Research. 10.1021/acs.jproteome.2c00120
    
  
  
    Developers
    
      
        SV
        Sander Van PuyveldeLead developer
      
    
  



  
    📂
    
      Thermo Raw File Parser
      File conversion
    
  
  
    Converts Thermo .raw files to open formats (mzML, MGF, or Parquet) on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Widely used as a preprocessing step in proteomics pipelines.
    
      ⬡ GitHub
    
    
      Hulstaert et al. (2020). ThermoRawFileParser: modular, scalable, and cross-platform RAW file conversion. Journal of Proteome Research. 10.1021/acs.jproteome.9b00328
    
  
  
    Developers
    
      
        NH
        Niels HulstaertLead developer
      
    
  



Taxonomy & Metaproteomics


  
    🌿
    
      Unipept
      Metaproteomics
    
  
  
    Tryptic peptide-based functional and taxonomic analysis of metaproteome samples. Exposes a public API handling millions of queries per month. Integrates with major proteomics workflows via Python and R clients.
    
      ⬡ GitHub
      Web app
      API docs
    
    
      Mesuere et al. (2012). Unipept: tryptic peptide-based biodiversity analysis of metaproteome samples. Journal of Proteome Research. 10.1021/pr300576s
    
  
  
    Developers
    
      
        BM
        Bart MesuereLead developer
      
      
        TV
        Tibo Vande MoorteleContributor
      
    
  



  
    🎲
    
      Peptonizer2000
      Taxonomy
    
  
  
    Provides probabilistic taxon confidence scores for metaproteomics using a Bayesian network approach. Propagates peptide-level evidence into organism-level posteriors, giving statistically grounded taxon assignments in complex microbial community samples.
    
      ⬡ GitHub
    
  
  
    Developers
    
      
        TV
        Tibo Vande MoorteleLead developer

title: Research
text: CompOmics is a bioinformatics research group at Ghent University and VIB, focused on computational proteomics. We develop open-source software and methods to push the boundaries of mass spectrometry-based proteomics.

title: Research
text: CompOmics is a bioinformatics research group at Ghent University and VIB, focused on computational proteomics. We develop open-source software and methods to push the boundaries of mass spectrometry-based proteomics.

title: Research
text:
Research Themes

Peptide & Protein Identification
Development of database search engines, post-processing tools, and validation strategies for PSM-level and protein-level identification in DDA and DIA workflows.


AI-Based Spectrum & Retention Time Prediction
We train and apply deep learning models to predict MS2 spectra, retention times, and other peptide properties, enabling data-independent analysis and rescoring workflows.


FAIR Proteomics Data
We contribute to community standards including HUPO-PSI formats (mzML, mzTab, mzSpecLib), ProteomeXchange/PRIDE data sharing, and ProteomicsML for machine learning-ready datasets.


Proteomics Data Infrastructure
Building scalable data formats (Parquet, Feather), APIs, and pipelines to make large-scale proteomics data accessible and reusable by the community.

title: Research
text:
Collaborations & Consortia

HUPO-PSI
ProteomeXchange
PRIDE
ProteomicsML

title: CompOmics
text:
Our Vision
CompOmics pushes the boundaries of computational proteomics at Ghent University and VIB. We believe that unlocking the full potential of mass spectrometry data requires tight integration of algorithmic innovation, machine learning, and open data standards.
Our mission is to develop open-source tools and community resources that make proteomics data analysis faster, more accurate, and accessible to researchers worldwide: from raw spectrum interpretation to FAIR-compliant data sharing.
We are committed to open science: all our software, datasets, and workflows are publicly available and built to last.








3 Research Lines


524 Publications


22 Tools


27 Members




  
    
    Tools
    Open-source software for proteomics data analysis
  
  
    
    Team
    Meet our researchers and developers
  
  
    
    Publications
    Our research output and scientific papers
  



  
    Where to find us
    Computational Omics and Systems Biology Group
    Department of Biomolecular Medicine, Ghent University
    VIB-UGent Center for Medical Biotechnology, VIB
    Technologiepark 759052 Gent-Zwijnaarde, Belgium
    E-mail: lennart.martens@UGent.be

Latest News
News items will appear here — see news/index.qmd.

Selected Tools
Tool highlights will appear here — see tools/index.qmd.

title: Tools & Software
text:
Prediction

  
    
      
      DeepLC
    
    Predicts when peptides will elute from the LC column during a mass spectrometry run. Unlike other tools, it handles modified peptides accurately, even modifications it has never seen before. When applied to a new instrument or experimental setup, it adapts quickly through transfer learning, requiring only a small number of example peptides rather than a full retraining.
    
       GitHub
       Docs
       PyPI
       Bioconda
       Web app
       Paper 2021
       Paper 2026
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Robbin Bouwmeester
     Ralf Gabriels
  


  
    
      
      MS²PIP
    
    Predicts fragment ion peak intensities from peptide sequence alone. These predictions are used to build in silico spectral libraries and to power the MS²Rescore rescoring pipeline. Fine-tuning for user-defined modifications is possible through MS²DIP.
    
       GitHub
       Web app
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Arthur Declercq
     Ralf Gabriels
  


  
    
      
      IM2Deep
    
    Predicts collisional cross-section (CCS) values for peptides including multiply modified ones. Supports ion mobility filtering in DDA and DIA workflows.
    
       GitHub
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Arthur Declercq
     Robbin Bouwmeester

title: Tools & Software
text:
Rescoring

  
    
      
      MS²Rescore
    
    Rescores peptide-spectrum matches by combining predicted LC-MS/MS features (retention time, fragment intensities, and ion mobility) with observed features. Substantially increases the number of confident identifications compared to search engine scores alone. Works with DDA and DIA data, and has been validated for immunopeptidomics and metaproteomics.
    
       GitHub
       Docs
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Ralf Gabriels
     Arthur Declercq

title: Tools & Software
text:
Data Standards & Utilities

  
    
      
      psm_utils
    
    A Python library for parsing, converting, and handling peptide-spectrum matches from any major search engine. Provides a unified PSM object model and format-agnostic I/O for building analysis pipelines.
    
       GitHub
       Docs
       PyPI
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Ralf Gabriels
  


  
    
      
      lesSDRF
    
    An interactive desktop app for annotating proteomics experiments in the SDRF format. Guides users through ontology-compliant metadata entry. HAMLET extends this with LLM-based pre-filling from manuscript text.
    
       GitHub
       Web app
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Sander Van Puyvelde
  


  
    
      
      Thermo Raw File Parser
    
    Converts Thermo .raw files to open formats (mzML, MGF, or Parquet) on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Widely used as a preprocessing step in proteomics pipelines.
    
       GitHub
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Robbin Bouwmeester

title: Tools & Software
text:
Taxonomy & Metaproteomics

  
    
      
      Unipept
    
    Tryptic peptide-based functional and taxonomic analysis of metaproteome samples. Exposes a public API handling millions of queries per month and integrates with major proteomics workflows via a Python and R client.
    
       GitHub
       Web app
       API docs
       Paper
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Robbin Bouwmeester
     Arthur Declercq
  


  
    
      
      Peptonizer2000
    
    Provides probabilistic taxon confidence scores for metaproteomics using a Bayesian network approach. Propagates peptide-level evidence into organism-level posteriors, giving statistically grounded taxon assignments in complex microbial community samples.
    
       GitHub
    
  
  
    CompOmics Developers
     Robbin Bouwmeester

title: Tools & Software
text:
Taxonomy & Metaproteomics

  
    
      
      Unipept
    
    Tryptic peptide-based functional and taxonomic analysis of metaproteome samples. Exposes a public API handling millions of queries per month and integrates with major proteomics workflows via a Python and R client.
    
       GitHub
       Web app
       API docs
       Paper

    Bart Mesuere
    Tibo Vande Moortele
  


  
    
      
      Peptonizer2000
    
    Provides probabilistic taxon confidence scores for metaproteomics using a Bayesian network approach. Propagates peptide-level evidence into organism-level posteriors, giving statistically grounded taxon assignments in complex microbial community samples.

Predicts when peptides will elute from the LC column during a mass spectrometry run. Unlike other tools, it handles modified peptides accurately, even modifications it has never seen before. When applied to a new instrument or experimental setup, it adapts quickly through transfer learning, requiring only a small number of example peptides rather than a full retraining.
Developers: Robbin Bouwmeester, Ralf Gabriels, Niels Hulstaert, Sven Degroeve
Links: GitHub · Docs · PyPI · Bioconda · Web app
Citation:

Bouwmeester et al. (2021). DeepLC can predict retention times for peptides that carry as-yet unseen modifications. Nature Methods. DOI: 10.1038/s41592-021-01301-5


Bouwmeester, Nameni et al. (2026). Transfer learning in DeepLC improves LC retention time prediction across substantially different modifications and setups. Nature Communications. DOI: 10.1038/s41467-026-68981-5



MS²PIP
Predicts fragment ion peak intensities from peptide sequence alone. These predictions are used to build in silico spectral libraries and to power the MS²Rescore rescoring pipeline. Fine-tuning for user-defined modifications is possible through MS²DIP.
Developers: Sven Degroeve, Arthur Declercq, Ralf Gabriels
Links: GitHub · Web app
Citation:

Degroeve & Martens (2013). MS²PIP: a tool for MS/MS peak intensity prediction. Bioinformatics. DOI: 10.1093/bioinformatics/btt355




IM2Deep
Predicts collisional cross-section (CCS) values for peptides including multiply modified ones. Supports ion mobility filtering in DDA and DIA workflows.
Developers: Arthur Declercq, Robbin Bouwmeester
Links: GitHub
Citation:

Declercq et al. (2024). IM2Deep: predicting collisional cross sections for multi-modified peptides. Journal of Proteome Research. DOI: 10.1021/acs.jproteome.4c00098

title: Tools & Software
text:
Rescoring

MS²Rescore
Rescores peptide-spectrum matches by combining predicted LC-MS/MS features (retention time, fragment intensities, and ion mobility) with observed features. Substantially increases the number of confident identifications compared to search engine scores alone. Works with DDA and DIA data, and has been validated for immunopeptidomics and metaproteomics.
Developers: Ralf Gabriels, Arthur Declercq
Links: GitHub · Docs
Citation:

Declercq et al. (2022). MS²Rescore: data-driven rescoring dramatically boosts immunopeptide identification rates. Molecular & Cellular Proteomics. DOI: 10.1016/j.mcpro.2022.100266

title: Tools & Software
text:
Data Standards & Utilities

psm_utils
A Python library for parsing, converting, and handling peptide-spectrum matches from any major search engine. Provides a unified PSM object model and format-agnostic I/O for building analysis pipelines.
Developers: Ralf Gabriels
Links: GitHub · Docs · PyPI



lesSDRF
An interactive desktop app for annotating proteomics experiments in the SDRF format. Guides users through ontology-compliant metadata entry. HAMLET extends this with LLM-based pre-filling from manuscript text.
Developers: Sander Van Puyvelde
Links: GitHub · Web app
Citation:

Van Puyvelde et al. (2022). lesSDRF: a desktop app for SDRF proteomics metadata annotation. Journal of Proteome Research. DOI: 10.1021/acs.jproteome.2c00120




Thermo Raw File Parser
Converts Thermo .raw files to open formats (mzML, MGF, or Parquet) on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Widely used as a preprocessing step in proteomics pipelines.
Developers: Niels Hulstaert
Links: GitHub
Citation:

Hulstaert et al. (2020). ThermoRawFileParser: modular, scalable, and cross-platform RAW file conversion. Journal of Proteome Research. DOI: 10.1021/acs.jproteome.9b00328

title: Tools & Software
text:
Taxonomy & Metaproteomics

Unipept
Tryptic peptide-based functional and taxonomic analysis of metaproteome samples. Exposes a public API handling millions of queries per month and integrates with major proteomics workflows via a Python and R client.
Developers: Bart Mesuere, Tibo Vande Moortele
Links: GitHub · Web app · API docs
Citation:

Mesuere et al. (2012). Unipept: tryptic peptide-based biodiversity analysis of metaproteome samples. Journal of Proteome Research. DOI: 10.1021/pr300576s




Peptonizer2000
Provides probabilistic taxon confidence scores for metaproteomics using a Bayesian network approach. Propagates peptide-level evidence into organism-level posteriors, giving statistically grounded taxon assignments in complex microbial community samples.
Developers: Tibo Vande Moortele
Links: GitHub
